message:
feat(cli): auto-stop conflicting systemd service before launching interactive TUI

When starting the interactive TUI server (`serve --tui`), the CLI now
detects whether the system's installed systemd user service
(agent-mail.service) is configured to listen on the same port, and
stops it automatically before binding.

Without this, launching the TUI on the default port would fail with
"address already in use" because the background systemd service holds
the port. Users had to manually run `systemctl --user stop
agent-mail.service` first — an easy-to-forget step that produced a
confusing error.

Implementation:

- conflicting_systemd_service_port(): reads the installed systemd
  unit file (~/.config/systemd/user/agent-mail.service), parses its
  ExecStart line, and extracts the --port value. Returns None on
  non-Linux or if the unit file doesn't exist / has no --port flag.

- maybe_stop_conflicting_managed_service(): called early in
  handle_serve_http() when tui_enabled is true. If the systemd
  service port matches the requested port, runs `systemctl --user
  stop agent-mail.service` and polls for port release (up to 6s).

- wait_for_port_release(): promoted from an inner function in
  kill_port_holder_with_pids to a module-level function so it can be
  reused by both the systemd-stop path and the existing PID-kill
  path. The inner copy is removed to eliminate duplication.

The systemd stop is only attempted for interactive TUI launches —
headless/background starts are unaffected, avoiding accidental
service disruption in automated deployments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
